channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: TTAP rejects reports of rift with Imran Khan’s family, reaffirms strong alliance
item.description: The Tehreek Tahaffuz Aiyeen-e-Pakistan (TTAP) has strongly rejected media reports suggesting any internal differences or rifts with the family of jailed former Prime Minister and PTI founder Imran Kha...

item.title: OWWA Seeks P12 Billion to Support Repatriation and Reintegration of OFWs Amid Middle East Crisis
item.description: The Overseas Workers Welfare Administration (OWWA) is requesting a P12-billion supplemental budget to support the repatriation and reintegration of overseas Filipino workers (OFWs) affected by the ong...

item.title: KIM Reviews TVET Closure Order Amid Student Concerns
item.description: The Kenya Institute of Management (KIM) has dismissed a closure notice issued by the Technical and Vocational Education and Training Authority (TVETA) on April 20, 2026, which revoked the institution'...

item.title: Engineering the World Cup Pitch: A Look at 2026's Turf Logistics
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup, co-hosted by the United States, Canada, and Mexico, presents unique logistical challenges for its playing surfaces. With 26 stadiums, many with varying climates and light cond...

item.title: Dean Young takes interim Dragons coaching role, affecting NSW Blues assistant position
item.description: Dean Young’s move to step into the interim head coaching role at the St George Illawarra Dragons has created a knock-on effect for the NSW State of Origin setup. According to the report, his new respo...

item.title: Odisha CEO directs verification of voter deletions after 9.8 lakh names flagged in SIR exercise
item.description: The Chief Electoral Officer (CEO) of Odisha, RS Gopalan, has directed electoral registration officers to carry out proper verification before removing any names from the voter rolls. This instruction ...

item.title: Woman charged with attempted murder following central London collision injuring pedestrians
item.description: A 29-year-old woman has been charged with attempted murder after a car struck pedestrians in central London in the early hours of Sunday morning, leaving multiple people injured, including one woman i...

item.title: Gor Mahia held by Bandari in goalless draw overshadowed by refereeing disputes and theft incident at Kasarani Stadium
item.description: Gor Mahia’s push for the 2025/2026 Kenyan Premier League title faced turbulence on Sunday after a goalless draw against Bandari FC at Kasarani Stadium. Despite the result extending Gor Mahia’s lead at...

item.title: FIA confirms in-season F1 regulation adjustments ahead of Miami Grand Prix
item.description: Formula One’s governing body, the FIA, together with teams and key stakeholders, has unanimously agreed to introduce a set of in-season rule refinements that will come into effect from the upcoming Mi...

item.title: Government delays Sh2 billion creators’ loan scheme to improve lending conditions
item.description: The Tanzanian government has postponed the rollout of a Sh2 billion loan facility intended for content creators and other young people in the creative industry, in order to review and improve the lend...

item.title: Big tech’s Australian data centre investment faces market test amid AI boom
item.description: The article examines the growing wave of investment in Australian data centres driven by global technology companies and the accelerating demand for artificial intelligence infrastructure. Despite ong...

item.title: Islamabad prepares security and logistics for expected US-Iran talks amid regional tensions
item.description: Authorities in Islamabad have started full-scale preparations for a possible second round of US-Iran peace talks, even though the final schedule has not yet been confirmed. The federal capital is witn...

item.title: APC releases timetable for 2027 elections primaries and announces nomination fees
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) has officially released its timetable and schedule of activities ahead of the 2027 general elections in Nigeria, outlining key dates for its internal primary electi...

item.title: BlackBerry shares jump after deepened collaboration with Nvidia
item.description: BlackBerry shares rose sharply on Monday following news of an expanded partnership with U.S. tech giant Nvidia, according to reporting by The Canadian Press. The company’s stock increased by approxima...

item.title: Tshwane CFO rejects allegations of improper influence in R2.9bn municipal security tender
item.description: Tshwane chief financial officer Gareth Mnisi has strongly denied allegations that he acted as a so-called “tender-fixer” in relation to a R2.9 billion municipal security contract. His testimony was gi...

item.title: US expands naval blockade on Iranian-linked shipping, raising legal and practical concerns
item.description: The United States has broadened its naval blockade against Iran, announcing it will target any vessel worldwide that is linked to Iranian trade or support, regardless of its location or flag. This mar...

item.title: UN and EU estimate $71.4 billion required for Gaza reconstruction over next decade after war devastation
item.description: According to a joint assessment by the United Nations and the European Union, Gaza will require approximately $71.4 billion over the next ten years for recovery and reconstruction following extensive ...

item.title: Pakistan Army Chief Munir urges dialogue and de-escalation during Iran visit amid Middle East tensions
item.description: Chief of Army Staff and Chief of Defence Forces Field Marshal Asim Munir recently concluded a three-day official visit to Iran, where he held high-level meetings aimed at strengthening regional stabil...

item.title: Arsenal’s Premier League Title Chances Remain Alive After Defeat to Manchester City
item.description: Arsenal’s hopes of winning the Premier League title have taken a hit after their defeat to Manchester City, but the race is still far from over. The Gunners, who have been leading the table since Octo...

item.title: Nigeria domestic debt rises to ₦80.49 trillion as government uses ₦100 billion from unclaimed funds
item.description: Nigeria’s domestic debt has climbed to ₦80.49 trillion, according to the Debt Management Office, with FGN Bonds making up the largest share at about 79% of the total. The latest development shows that...
